mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over all 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5%, labor market slack triggers recession fears that override earnings growth, causing sustained negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in over 55% of periods where UNRATE crosses 5.5%, or signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which directly boosts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST both rise, or the interaction term loses statistical significance with p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: During inflation disinflation phases, falling CPIAUCSL combined with stable FEDFUNDS reduces real borrowing costs and improves equity risk premiums, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses or becomes statistically insignificant when CPIAUCSL declines while FEDFUNDS remains unchanged, or 5-day forward returns show negative mean during disinflation.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in the industrial production index reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and output, which directly correlate with higher corporate profit margins and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.03 across all rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payrolls additions exceeding 200,000 signal robust economic momentum that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases by > 200k in a single month
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative in over 60% of months where PAYEMS exceeds the 200k threshold, or signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth decelerates while inflation remains elevated, stagflation dynamics compress equity valuations and trigger negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint condition of falling GDPC1 and rising CPIAUCSL fails to predict negative returns, or the interaction effect shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample forecasting.

mechanism_narrative: In post-shock recovery regimes, rising industrial production alongside falling unemployment indicates successful economic stabilization, which rapidly boosts market sentiment and generates positive 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power or reverses direction during recovery phases, or 5-day forward returns show no significant positive mean when INDPRO rises and UNRATE falls.
